exemplar.title: How does Daniel Keyes Use Dreams and Hallucinations to Reveal Charlieâs Inner Struggles Throughout his Transformations in Flowers for Algernon?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Daniel Keyes Use Dreams and Hallucinations to Reveal Charlieâs Inner Struggles Throughout his Transformations in Flowers for Algernon?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Topic and purpose are communicated accurately and effectively with sustained focus throughout the introduction and body.
The research question is clearly stated, precisely focused, and consistently embedded in the structure of the essay.
Analytical sections repeatedly link back to the research question, demonstrating coherent guidance of the investigation.
weaknesses: Occasional reliance on plot summary in the opening and some body paragraphs distracts from analytical focus.
The methodology section is brief and lacks detailed justification of source selection criteria.
Shifts in analytical approach (e.g. from dream analysis to memory recall) are not fully explained in methodological terms.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Focus and Method is awarded 5/6. The work communicates its topic and research question with clarity and precision (band 5), and maintains focus throughout most of the essay. However, the methodological description is somewhat limited in breadth and rigour (band 4). On balance, the exceptional clarity and coherence of topic and question outweigh the methodological gaps, justifying a high-band score of 5.

strengths: Demonstrates a coherent and accurate understanding of Flowers for Algernon through well-chosen episodes and quotations.
Uses literary and psychoanalytic terminology (e.g., metaphor, free association, polysyndeton) mostly accurately to frame the analysis.
Integrates primary quotations effectively to support interpretations of Charlieâs psychological state.
weaknesses: Frequent plot retelling reduces opportunities for deeper critical application of knowledge.
Engagement with secondary scholarship is uneven, with some sources not fully integrated or critically weighed.
Occasional imprecision in technical terms (e.g., slight misuse of âepithetâ) indicates inconsistent application of disciplinary vocabulary.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge and Understanding is awarded 4/6. The essay shows good knowledge of the primary text and applies subject-specific terminology accurately in most places (band 4). However, persistent narrative summary and patchy engagement with secondary sources limit the overall effectiveness, placing it solidly in the 3-4 band.

strengths: Offers consistently perceptive close readings of key dream and hallucination passages, linking imagery to Charlieâs inner conflicts.
Supports many analytical points with well-chosen textual quotations and occasional theoretical framing.
Demonstrates moments of strong insight (e.g., connecting the bakery dream to childhood memory, allusion to Platoâs Cave).
weaknesses: Research breadth is limited: important scholarly debates on the novel are largely absent and non-academic sources are sometimes relied upon.
Analysis sometimes lapses into unsupported speculative leaps or extended summary without sufficient follow-through.
Critical evaluation of sources is minimal; counter-arguments and alternative interpretations are rarely acknowledged.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical Thinking is awarded 7/12. The essay delivers generally sound and occasionally excellent analysis (analysis strand mid-band) and constructs a coherent argument, but research relevance and discussion/evaluation are only adequate (bands 4â5). The mixture of strong close readings with shortcomings in breadth, source evaluation, and balance warrants a mid-lower band placement, corresponding to a total of 7 marks.

strengths: Structure meets all essential requirements: title page, table of contents, clear headings, introduction, body, conclusion, and works-cited list.
Page numbers, word count and title placement are all correctly applied, aiding navigation and readability.
Font, spacing and margins are consistent, and headings/subheadings guide the reader effectively.
weaknesses: Citation formatting (MLA italics, punctuation, hyperlinks) is inconsistently applied throughout the Works Cited and in-text.
Table of contents page numbers do not fully correspond to actual content, requiring verification.
Transitions between some sections are abrupt, and occasional paragraph density hinders readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is awarded 3/4. The essay fulfills the structural and formatting requirements with only minor lapses in consistency (band 3). Although most presentation elements are correctly applied, the inconsistent citation style and navigational inaccuracies prevent a top-band score.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Method stated but somewhat limited in breadth and rigour.** The student outlines reliance on close textual analysis supplemented by "secondary sources from both literary and psychological viewpoints," naming Freud as the principal framework. This constitutes a recognisable methodology and shows some informed source selection.  
â¢ However, the range of secondary material is narrow (five works cited, most lightly used, with one popular blog). There is little evidence of systematic criteria for source selection or critical comparison of differing scholarly views.  
â¢ Documentation conventions are generally followed, yet the description of method is brief and the application occasionally slips into plot summary rather than sustained analytical method. These limitations place the work in the upper half of the 3-4 band.

**Most presentation elements are in place, with small lapses.** The essay provides a word count, a clear title, page numbers and a functional table of contents. In-text citations and a works-cited list follow a recognisable style, though there are occasional inconsistencies in punctuation and hyperlink display.  
â¢ Font choice, spacing and margins appear standard and aid readability; headings are consistently formatted.  
â¢ A few minor formatting slips (e.g., mixed header styles, inconsistent italics/quotation usage, some long quotations not fully formatted) prevent full compliance with "correctly applied" criteria, placing the work at a 3 within the 3-4 band.

**Research is adequate but limited in breadth.** The essay cites the primary text and five secondary sources, most of which are relevant (Freud, psychoanalytic criticism, symbolism studies). The sources are generally connected to the RQ, indicating purposeful selection.  
â¢ However, the literary criticism is thin for an EE; key scholarly debates on *Flowers for Algernon* are largely absent, and several citations (e.g., blog post) lack academic rigour.  
â¢ Consequently, while research supports the investigation in a basic way, its depth and comprehensive relevance are only partial, aligning with the lower end of the 4-6 band.

**Clear, explicit topic and purpose.** The title, introductory paragraph and opening sentences make it immediately apparent that the essay will investigate Daniel Keyesâ use of Charlieâs dreams and hallucinations to expose his inner conflicts before, during and after intellectual transformation.  
â¢ The scope (only one novel, one character, two related literary devices) is **appropriate and manageable**, showing an effective narrowing of focus.  
â¢ Minor issues remain â wording of the title is slightly awkward and the discussion occasionally strays into broad plot summary â but overall the topic is communicated with accuracy and sustained focus, meriting the upper-band score.

**Reasoned discussion present but evaluation is limited.** The structure moves logically from one thematic strand to the next, and the student attempts to build an argument about the progressive revelation of Charlieâs inner struggle.  
â¢ However, evaluation of sources and acknowledgment of limitations are minimal, and the concluding reflections remain largely descriptive rather than critically weighing significance or implications.  
â¢ The argumentâs focus wavers due to repetition and over-summary, and synthesis between primary and secondary material is uneven. These qualities match the descriptor of an "adequate argument with some inconsistencies and basic evaluation," meriting a 4 in the 4-6 band.

**Analysis shows insight but is uneven.** The student offers thoughtful close readings of key dream and hallucination episodes, linking imagery (blood, masks, mirrors, caves) to Charlieâs psychological conflicts. Many points are supported with pertinent textual quotation and some theoretical framing, demonstrating an emerging analytical voice.  
â¢ Nevertheless, sections often lapse into lengthy narrative recount or speculative psychoanalytic leaps that are not fully substantiated by evidence. Counter-arguments or alternative interpretations are rarely acknowledged, reducing critical sharpness.  
â¢ Overall, the essay delivers generally sound, sometimes perceptive analysis with mostly supported conclusions, positioning it mid-band (score 5) within the 4-6 range.

**Meets formal structural requirements effectively.** The essay contains a title page with required details, an organized table of contents, distinct sections (introduction, methodology, main body, conclusion) and a works-cited list following MLA conventions.  
â¢ Headings and subheadings guide the reader, and the progression of ideas is generally logical, fulfilling the expectations for EE structure.  
â¢ Minor issuesâoccasional abrupt transitions and some redundancyâare present but do not compromise overall clarity, justifying a top-band score of 4 for structure.

**Demonstrates good but not consistently excellent knowledge of the novel and context.** The essay selects numerous relevant episodes and quotations, correctly situating them in the narrative arc and using psychoanalytic ideas (dream symbolism, free association) to interpret them.  
â¢ Understanding of *Flowers for Algernon* is generally coherent and accurate; the student shows awareness of character development, narrative voice and thematic concerns.  
â¢ Nevertheless, reliance on plot retelling is frequent, and engagement with secondary scholarship is patchy. Sources are cited but not always fully integrated or critically weighed, so application of knowledge is only partially effective, warranting a solid 4 within the 3-4 band.

**Terminology is mostly accurate and appropriate.** The student employs literary and psychoanalytic terms such as "metaphor," "polysyndeton," "free association," and "symbolise" with reasonable correctness, helping to frame the discussion within accepted disciplinary discourse.  
â¢ Technical vocabulary is used in most sections, and references to Freud, symbolism, and narrative technique enrich the analysis.  
â¢ Occasional imprecision (e.g., confusing character names, slight misuse of âepithetâ) and uneven explanation of theoretical terms prevent full consistency, but overall usage aligns with the 3-4 bandâs descriptor of "mostly accurate" application. A score of 4 reflects the upper end of that range.

**Research question is precise, focused and well embedded.** The student formulates a single, literary-stylistic question (âHow does Daniel Keyes use Charlieâs dreams and hallucinationsâ¦?â) that is clearly linked to the chosen category and to subsequent chapters.  
â¢ Each analytical section (Desire for Intelligence, Fear Towards Women, etc.) is explicitly connected back to this question, demonstrating that the RQ guides the organisation of the essay.  
â¢ Some paragraphs drift into thematic discussion (e.g., lengthy plot retelling of bakery events), preventing perfect consistency of focus, yet the overall alignment between RQ and discussion remains strong, justifying a solid 5 in the 5-6 band.

annotations.comment: The research question is stated succinctly and guides the entire essay, providing clear direction for the analysis of dreams and hallucinations throughout Charlieâs transformations.
Including an exact word count at the start ensures compliance with formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to detail.
The table of contents is logically structured but the listed page numbers do not correspond with the actual content pages; please verify and update for accurate navigation.
The opening paragraph relies heavily on plot summary; consider condensing narrative details to focus more quickly on analytical framing and the investigationâs purpose.
Insightfully connects Charlieâs bakery dream to childhood memory, demonstrating strong analytical linkage between dream content and character development.
The introduction embeds the research question effectively within the context and explains its significance, providing a purposeful link to the essayâs analytical focus.

The methodology outlines use of Freudâs dream analysis but lacks specific criteria for source selection and comparison; expand this section to justify why particular secondary materials were chosen.
Relying almost exclusively on Freud limits the depth of psychoanalytic engagement; incorporate additional theoretical perspectives to enrich and balance your analysis.
The metaphor of the knife and power is insightful but presented in one dense block; break into clearer subpoints to improve readability.
Consistently links dream imagery back to the research question, maintaining strong focus on how hallucinations reveal Charlieâs conflicts.
The transition into the next section is abrupt; add a brief linking sentence to guide the reader smoothly between themes.
Several quotations lack follow-up analysis; ensure each citation is explicitly interpreted to support your argument.
Compelling visual analysis of the painting contrast, which deepens understanding of Charlieâs identity conflict.

The title clearly communicates the investigationâs focus on dreams and hallucinations, effectively narrowing the topic to Charlieâs inner conflicts in Flowers for Algernon, which establishes a precise and sustained purpose.
This close reading of Charlieâs first dream effectively links symbolic blood imagery to his fear of punishment, supported by well-chosen quotations.
The definition of free association is clear, but the integration of the Sosnoski source is superficial; deepen engagement with the theory to strengthen your psychoanalytic interpretation.
Sections occasionally revert to narrative recount; ensure each quotation is followed by interpretation rather than extended summary.
Excellent use of polysyndeton terminology and analysis of repetition, which deepens understanding of Charlieâs emotional state.
The term âepithetâ is misused here; double-check literary terminology and ensure each term is applied accurately within its established definition.
Your analysis relies on a single Freudian view; introduce contrasting psychoanalytic or feminist critiques to demonstrate critical evaluation of sources.
Effective integration of Keyesâs quote on fear towards women, which vividly illustrates Charlieâs anxiety in the dream sequence.
Consider weaving the water symbolism in the drowning dream back to earlier water motifs for greater thematic cohesion.
The metaphor of intelligence as âthe thingsâ is particularly effective, illustrating the tension between Charlieâs intellectual growth and emotional isolation.
Interpretation of the cake figures is intriguing but would benefit from theoretical support beyond psychoanalysis to broaden your critical perspective.
The shift from dream analysis to memory recall is not justified methodologically; clarify in your method section how and why this change occurs.
The allusion to Platoâs Cave is persuasive and enriches the analysis by connecting Charlieâs dilemma with broader philosophical discourse.
This paragraph covers multiple pointsâconsider splitting it to improve readability and to emphasize each analytical insight.
The conclusion effectively summarizes findings but omits a critical reflection on methodological limitations; include an evaluative comment on your approachâs scope.
Memory analysis is well supported by quotations but lacks engagement with secondary scholarship at this stage; integrate a critical source here.
Your hallucination analysis demonstrates strong depth, balancing textual evidence with conceptual insight.
Expand your rhetorical analysis of the mirror scenes by applying specific terms (e.g., metonymy, anagnorisis) to enhance precision.
The final generalizations introduce new ideas on literary dreams; restrict the conclusion to insights directly derived from your investigation.
Citation formatting is inconsistentâensure uniform application of MLA italics, punctuation, and hyperlink conventions throughout the Works Cited.
Inclusion of a non-peer-reviewed blog limits academic rigour; prioritize scholarly, peer-reviewed sources to strengthen your reference list.
